Problem Statement

India Faces a Severe Coordination Gap in Emergency Healthcare

No Real-Time Visibility

No centralized system exists to track blood availability. Hospitals are blind to what nearby blood banks have in stock.

Manual Coordination

During emergencies, staff rely on frantic manual phone calls to 5-10 different centers while the patient waits.

Siloed Operations

Blood banks operate independently with no data sharing, leading to wastage in one center and shortage in another.

The Consequence

Critical Delays

Every minute spent searching for blood is a minute delayed in surgery.

"Preventable deaths occur daily not because resources don't exist, but because of resource discovery time."

During emergencies, Time = Life.

Current System Is:

Fragmented

Manual

Slow

Non-transparent
